operating_state: Total revenue reached $6.6 billion in Q2 2022, up 70% year-over-year, driven primarily by the Xilinx acquisition completed in February 2022. Data Center segment increased 83% from higher EPYC server processor sales, Client segment grew 25% on strong Ryzen mobile processor demand with higher average selling prices, and Gaming segment rose 32% on discrete GPU and semi-custom revenue strength. International sales comprised 70% of total revenue, with the company generating $2.0 billion in operating cash flow for the six-month period despite net working capital outflows.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed to 46% from 48% in the prior year quarter due to acquisition-related intangible amortization and acquired inventory fair value step-up costs from the Xilinx transaction. Operating expenses increased substantially driven by acquisition-related intangible amortization and headcount expansion for product development. Manufacturing yields and wafer purchase commitments with GLOBALFOUNDRIES through 2025 continue to impact per-unit costs.
material_events: AMD completed the $48.8 billion Xilinx acquisition in February 2022, issuing 429 million shares, followed by the Pensando Systems acquisition in May 2022. The company issued $1.0 billion in new senior notes in June 2022, established a $3.0 billion revolving credit facility, and repurchased $2.8 billion of common stock during the six-month period. AMD amended its wafer supply agreement with GLOBALFOUNDRIES, removing exclusivity commitments while maintaining minimum annual capacity allocations through 2025.
macro_exposures: The company maintains 70% international revenue exposure with manufacturing concentrated in Asia, utilizing forward contracts to hedge foreign currency fluctuations. Export restrictions intensified with Chinese entities added to Entity List, while new sanctions against Russia, Belarus and Ukraine regions create additional compliance requirements. Cryptocurrency mining demand volatility impacts GPU revenue with regulatory restrictions in multiple jurisdictions, while COVID-19 pandemic continues creating supply chain disruptions.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued integration benefits from the Xilinx acquisition across data center and embedded markets with expanded product portfolio enabling hardware acceleration opportunities. The company believes current cash position plus available credit facilities and operating cash flows will fund operations and purchase commitments over the next 12 months. Product roadmap focuses on next-generation processors and FPGAs with advanced node production concentrated at TSMC.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces measurement period adjustments for both Xilinx and Pensando acquisitions with purchase price allocations subject to revision within one year of closing dates. Ongoing patent litigation involves multiple jurisdictions with mixed outcomes and appeals pending, alongside total debt with covenant restrictions including minimum interest coverage requirements. AMD is upgrading its enterprise resource planning system creating implementation risks, while deferred tax liabilities related to acquired intangible assets increased due to utilization of tax attributes subject to potential limitations.
